school_activities__sports_involvement.school_activities_sports_involvement_inc:
National Honor Society (Member, 2023–present): Inducted spring 2023 based on GPA and teacher recommendations. I attend biweekly meetings, participate in peer tutoring in math and computer science, and regularly volunteer at school-organized service events such as campus clean-up days and supply drives.

Robotics Club (Programming Lead, 2022–present): Joined the Southridge Robotics Club sophomore year and quickly gravitated toward the programming subteam. I became Programming Lead at the beginning of junior year (fall 2023). I coordinate a small group of four students to write and test code for our competition robot using Java and the WPILib framework. In the 2023–2024 season, I helped rewrite our autonomous navigation code, which contributed to our team qualifying for the state-level competition for the first time in five years (March 2024).

Girls Who Code Chapter (Founding Member and Co-President, 2023–present): Helped launch our school’s Girls Who Code chapter in fall 2023 after noticing that most advanced CS classes were male-dominated. As Co-President, I help plan weekly meetings, organize beginner-friendly coding workshops, and coordinate guest speakers from local tech companies in Portland. We created a semester-long project where members built simple websites about topics they care about, and I mentored three freshmen through learning HTML, CSS, and basic JavaScript.

Varsity Girls’ Tennis (Varsity Player, 2022–present): Played JV as a freshman and moved up to varsity in my sophomore year. I typically play doubles and practice five days a week during the spring season. Our team placed second in our district in 2023, and I received the “Most Improved Player” award that season. I also attend optional off-season conditioning and weekend hitting sessions.

Math Club (Member, 2021–present): Attend weekly problem-solving sessions and participate in competitions such as the AMC 12 and regional math contests. I especially enjoy combinatorics and probability problems. In 2023, I placed in the top 10% of participants at our regional math competition hosted by Portland State University.

Student Council (Junior Class Representative, 2023–2024): Elected by my peers to serve as a junior class representative. Responsibilities include helping plan school events such as Homecoming and Winter Formal, assisting with fundraising (e.g., running concession stands at basketball games), and representing student concerns at monthly meetings with the administration. I co-led a small initiative to improve recycling signage around campus, which involved designing posters and coordinating with the custodial staff.

School Newspaper – The Southridge Sentinel (Staff Writer, 2022–present): Write 1–2 articles per month, mostly in the features and technology sections. I have covered topics such as the impact of AI tools on student learning, profiles of teachers who run unique clubs, and recaps of major school events. This role has helped improve my interviewing skills and ability to explain technical topics in an accessible way.

Awards and Honors:
- Honor Roll (2021–present): Earned Honor Roll status every semester since freshman year for maintaining a GPA above 3.5.
- AP Computer Science A Award (2023): Recognized by the CS department for having the highest score in the class average on programming projects and the final exam.
- Most Improved Player – Girls’ Tennis (2023): Voted on by teammates and coaches for significant improvement in skills and match performance over the season.

civic_and_community_involvement.civic_and_community_involvement:
Beaverton Public Library Teen Volunteer (2022–present): I volunteer approximately 6–8 hours per month at the Beaverton City Library, mostly on Saturday afternoons. My responsibilities include shelving books, preparing craft kits for children’s programs, helping set up and take down chairs and tables for events, and occasionally assisting at the information desk with simple tasks like directing visitors to the correct floor or section. One of my favorite recurring assignments is helping with the monthly STEM Storytime, where I help younger kids (ages 5–8) with simple science activities such as building paper rockets or making slime. I enjoy seeing how excited they get when an experiment works, and it has helped me practice explaining science concepts in kid-friendly language.

Coding Tutor – Community Center Workshops (2023–present): In partnership with our school’s Girls Who Code chapter and the local community center, I volunteer as a coding tutor for a free after-school program aimed at middle school students from underrepresented backgrounds in tech. We meet once a week during the school year. I help a group of 6–8 students learn basic programming concepts using Scratch and later Python. I’ve helped students build simple games like a maze runner and basic animations. I also stay after sessions to answer individual questions and sometimes help troubleshoot their home laptops so they can continue practicing on their own.

Food Bank Volunteer – Oregon Food Bank (2021–present): My family and I have been volunteering at the Oregon Food Bank warehouse a few times a year since my freshman year, usually on Saturday mornings. We help sort and repackage donated food, label boxes, and sometimes assemble produce bags. During summer 2023, I signed up for additional shifts on my own and completed roughly 20 hours between June and August. It was eye-opening to see the scale of need in our area and how many families rely on these services.

Neighborhood Mutual Aid / Little Free Pantry Organizer (2022–present): During the pandemic, a neighbor set up a small free pantry on our block, and I started helping maintain it in 2022. I check the pantry 2–3 times a week to restock canned goods, pasta, cereal, and basic toiletries that my family and a few neighbors donate. I also created a simple Google Sheet to coordinate contributions so we don’t end up with too much of one item and not enough of others. Around holidays, I put up flyers inviting more neighbors to contribute, and I’ve seen participation grow steadily.

Church Youth Group – Community Service Projects (2021–present): Although my family is not very formal about religion, we attend a local Vietnamese-American church that organizes youth group service events. Through this group, I have participated in park clean-ups, holiday gift drives for families in need, and occasional visits to a local senior center where we play board games with residents and help them with small tech tasks (like figuring out their phone settings). I help translate simple conversations between English and Vietnamese when needed, especially for older community members.

Voter Registration and Civic Engagement (2024): Even though I am not yet old enough to vote, I joined a nonpartisan youth-led civic engagement initiative in early 2024. We tabled at community events and in front of grocery stores to encourage eligible adults to register to vote and to check their registration status. I mostly helped with logistical tasks such as setting up the table, handing out flyers, and directing people to the official registration website using QR codes. This experience made me more aware of the importance of local elections and informed participation in democracy.

employment_record.employment_record_if_any:
Math and Computer Science Tutor (Self-Employed) – Beaverton, OR
June 2023 – present
I started offering private tutoring to middle and early high school students in my neighborhood after my sophomore year. I currently tutor 3–4 students per week, usually for 1-hour sessions, focusing on Algebra I, Geometry, and introductory programming (Scratch and Python). I advertise mainly through word of mouth and a simple flyer I posted on our neighborhood Facebook group. My responsibilities include preparing customized practice problems, reviewing homework, helping students prepare for tests, and sending short progress summaries to parents every few weeks. This experience has strengthened my communication skills and taught me how to break down complex concepts into smaller, understandable steps. I earn around $20 per hour and save most of my income for college.

Summer Camp Assistant – Beaverton Parks & Recreation
June 2024 – August 2024
During the summer before my senior year, I worked as a part-time assistant at a Beaverton Parks & Recreation day camp program for children ages 7–11. I worked approximately 20 hours per week. My duties included helping set up daily activities, supervising small groups of campers during field games and arts-and-crafts sessions, assisting with sign-in and sign-out, and making sure children followed safety rules (especially during outdoor time and water play days). I also helped lead a simple “Intro to Coding” activity one week, using block-based coding games on Chromebooks. This job required patience, energy, and strong communication with both kids and parents. It also gave me a better understanding of time management and responsibility, since I had to balance work hours with an online summer class.

Freelance Web Design for Local Nail Salon (Project-Based)
February 2024 – April 2024
A family friend who owns a small Vietnamese nail salon in Portland asked if I could help them improve their online presence. Over the course of about two months, I met with the owner on weekends to understand what information she wanted to share (services, pricing, photos, and booking info). I used a website builder (Wix) to design a simple, mobile-friendly site that included a gallery of work, Google Maps integration, and a contact form. I also created a basic Google Business profile and showed her how to update hours and respond to reviews. Although this was a one-time project rather than a regular job, it was my first experience working with a client and delivering something on a deadline, and it confirmed my interest in using technology to support small businesses.
